content_angles.angle: Terraform, Bicep, and ARM all claim to be 'the' way to do IaC on Azure -- most teams pick one without ever seeing the tradeoffs side by side
The Kubernetes certification path is confusing because every role needs a different sequence -- KCNA, CKA, CKS, KCAD all solve different problems
A CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions looks simple until you have to show the failure paths, not just the happy path
Most 'multi-agent' marketing systems are one big prompt -- a real dispatch/verdict swarm needs a hard MRR floor or it never stops shipping bad ideas
HITL gates get built as an afterthought, then teams wonder why nobody trusts the automation -- the gate has to be the loudest part of the diagram, not the smallest
RAG pipelines get pitched as 'just add a vector database' -- the actual system is a context injection pipeline with a real retrieval step

idea_reservoir.text: Comparing Azure services to their AWS equivalents is the fastest way to onboard someone who already knows one cloud but not the other
Multi-agent swarm governance only works if the human-in-the-loop gate can actually reject a proposal, not just rubber-stamp it
Layering an agentic system (infra, data, compute, model, agent execution, UI) the same way you'd layer any distributed system makes the design decisions obvious

kelvin_voice_profile.tone: Direct, grounded, practitioner first-person. No motivational poster energy.
kelvin_voice_profile.recent_focus: Multi-cloud IaC, agentic pipelines with HITL gates, building in public while employed full-time

build_updates.text: Shipped the Gemini image-gen pipeline this week -- every LinkedIn post now gets its own custom technical diagram instead of a stock photo, and it's wired straight into the HITL review queue
